KELOMPOK 8 PDS

2. Muhamad Alfarizi (1314623041)

1. Scrapping Dataset

In [ ]:
#@title Twitter Auth Token

twitter_auth_token = 'bc3ed24236285275482d46bcba75238572be6937'
In [ ]:
# ==============================
# 2. Install dependencies
# ==============================
!pip install pandas --quiet

# Install Node.js 20
!sudo apt-get update -y
!sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg

!sudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings
!curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/gpgkey/nodesource-repo.gpg.key | \
    sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg

!echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg] \
https://deb.nodesource.com/node_20.x nodistro main" | \
    sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/nodesource.list

!sudo apt-get update
!sudo apt-get install -y nodejs

# Install tweet-harvest
!npm install -g tweet-harvest

In [ ]:
# ==============================
# 3. Scraping Tweet
# ==============================
filename = 'dataset_kai_tumbler.csv'
search_keyword = 'tumbler kai lang:id'
limit = 2000

!npx -y tweet-harvest@2.6.1 \
    -o "{filename}" \
    -s "{search_keyword}" \
    --tab "LATEST" \
    -l {limit} \
    --token {twitter_auth_token}
[1G[0K⠙[1G[0K⠹[1G[0K⠸[1G[0K⠼[1G[0K⠴[1G[0K⠦[1G[0K[1m[32mTweet Harvest [v2.6.1][39m[22m [1m[32m[39m[22m [34mResearch by [39m[1m[34mHelmi Satria[39m[22m[34m[39m [34mUse it for Educational Purposes only![39m [34m[39m [33mThis script uses Chromium Browser to crawl data from Twitter with [1myour Twitter auth token[22m.[39m [33mPlease enter your Twitter auth token when prompted.[39m [33m[39m [31m[1mNote:[22m[39m Keep your access token secret! Don't share it with anyone else. [31m[1mNote:[22m[39m This script only runs on your local device. [34m[39m [34mOpening twitter search page...[39m [34m[39m [34m[39m [34mFound existing file ./tweets-data/dataset_kai_tumbler.csv, renaming to ./tweets-data/dataset_kai_tumbler.old.csv[39m [90m[39m [90m-- Scrolling... # ==============================
# 4. Cek Jumlah Data
# ==============================
import pandas as pd

# Update the filename to include the correct path
filename = '/content/tweets-data/dataset_kai_tumbler.csv'

df = pd.read_csv(filename)
print("Jumlah tweet:", len(df))

df.head(10)

2. Preprocessing

In [ ]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
In [ ]:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("df_kai_tumbler.csv", encoding='latin1')
df.head()
conversation_id_str created_at favorite_count full_text id_str image_url in_reply_to_screen_name lang location quote_count reply_count retweet_count tweet_url user_id_str username

In [ ]:

In [ ]:
import re
import string
import demoji

# Pastikan demoji aktif
demoji.download_codes()

# 1. Remove HTML tags
def remove_html(text):
    return re.sub(r'<.*?>', '', text)

# 2. Remove URLs
def remove_url(text):
    return re.sub(r'http\S+|www\.\S+', '', text)

# 3. Remove mentions (@user)
def remove_mention(text):
    return re.sub(r'@\S+', '', text)

# 4. Remove hashtag symbol but keep the word
def remove_hashtag(text):
    return re.sub(r'#(\w+)', r'\1', text)

# 5. Remove emojis
def remove_emoji(text):
    return demoji.replace(text, repl="")

# 6. Remove retweet markers (RT)
def remove_rt(text):
    return re.sub(r'\brt[\s:]+', '', text)

# 7. Remove multiple spaces
def remove_extra_spaces(text):
    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()

# MASTER FUNCTION (khusus untuk BERT)
def preprocess_for_bert(text):
    text = str(text).lower()
    text = remove_html(text)
    text = remove_url(text)
    text = remove_mention(text)
    text = remove_hashtag(text)
    text = remove_emoji(text)
    text = remove_rt(text)
    text = remove_extra_spaces(text)

    return text
/tmp/ipython-input-3158245292.py:6: FutureWarning: The demoji.download_codes attribute is deprecated and will be removed from demoji in a future version. It is an unused attribute as emoji codes are now distributed directly with the demoji package. demoji.download_codes()

slang_dict = {
    "muke": "muka",
    "jg": "juga",
    "gk": "gak", "ga": "gak", "ngga": "gak", "nggak": "gak", "kagak": "gak",
    "tdk": "tidak", "tkt": "takut", "tp": "tapi", "tpi": "tapi", "tbh": "jujur",
    "jg": "juga", "jga": "juga", "jd": "jadi", "jdi": "jadi",
    "udh": "sudah", "udah": "sudah", "sdh": "sudah",
    "blm": "belum", "blum": "belum",
    "bgt": "banget", "bgd": "banget", "bngt": "banget",
    "pls": "tolong", "plis": "tolong",
    "skr": "sekarang", "skrg": "sekarang",
    "kmrn": "kemarin", "kmrn2": "kemarin",
    "dr": "dari", "drpd": "daripada", "drpd": "daripada",
    "sm": "sama", "sma": "sama",
    "sy": "saya", "aq": "aku", "ak": "aku", "gue": "saya", "gua": "saya", "gw": "saya",
    "km": "kamu", "kmu": "kamu", "loe": "kamu", "lo": "kamu", "lu": "kamu",
    "trs": "terus", "trus": "terus", "trs2": "terus",
    "krn": "karena", "karna": "karena",
    "bs": "bisa", "bsa": "bisa",
    "yg": "yang", "yng": "yang",
    "kl": "kalau", "klo": "kalau", "klu": "kalau", "kalo": "kalau",
    "dgn": "dengan", "dg": "dengan",
    "aja": "saja", "aj": "saja",
    "dpt": "dapat", "dapet": "dapat",
    "pt": "perusahaan", "ptn": "perguruan tinggi negeri",
    "ntr": "nanti", "ntar": "nanti", "nnti": "nanti",
    "wkwk": "haha", "wkwkwk": "haha", "wk": "haha",
    "lol": "haha", "lmao": "haha",
    "pantes": "pantas",
    "bru": "baru", "br": "baru",
    "org": "orang",
    "smpe": "sampai", "sampe": "sampai",
    "ampe": "sampai",
    "ngap": "kenapa", "knp": "kenapa",
    "dlu": "dulu",
    "trnyata": "ternyata",
    "ampe": "sampai",
    "capek": "lelah", "cape": "lelah",
    "muke": "muka", "muka": "muka",
    "anj": "anjing", "anjg": "anjing",
    "btw": "ngomong-ngomong",
    "cm": "cuma", "cuma": "cuma", "cman": "cuma",
    "sbnr": "sebenarnya", "sbenernya": "sebenarnya",
    "goblok": "bodoh", "gblk": "bodoh",
    "pc": "komputer", "lptp": "laptop",
    "nyesek": "sedih", "sakit": "sakit",
}

def normalize_slang(text):
    words = text.split()
    return " ".join(slang_dict.get(w, w) for w in words)
In [ ]:
df1["clean_text"] = df1["full_text"].apply(preprocess_for_bert)
df1["clean_text"] = df1["clean_text"].apply(normalize_slang)
df1
full_text created_at clean_text

from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForSequenceClassification, pipeline
from transformers import TrainingArguments, Trainer
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
from datasets import Dataset
import pandas as pd
import torch
In [ ]:
if torch.cuda.is_available():
    device = torch.device("cuda")
    print("there are %d GPU(s) available", torch.cuda.device_count())

else:
    print("No GPU available ")
    device = torch.device("cpu")
No GPU available
In [ ]:
from transformers import pipeline
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForSequenceClassification

pretrained= "w11wo/indonesian-roberta-base-sentiment-classifier"

model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(pretrained)
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(pretrained)

labeler = pipeline("sentiment-analysis", model=model, tokenizer=tokenizer)

label_map = {'positive': 'positif', 'neutral': 'netral', 'negative': 'negatif'}
/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/huggingface_hub/utils/_auth.py:94: UserWarning: The secret `HF_TOKEN` does not exist in your Colab secrets. To authenticate with the Hugging Face Hub, create a token in your settings tab (https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens), set it as secret in your Google Colab and restart your session. You will be able to reuse this secret in all of your notebooks. Please note that authentication is recommended but still optional to access public models or datasets. warnings.warn(

0 Thu Nov 27 09:42:14 +0000 2025 apa ya tujuan dia viralin sesuatu yang belum j... negatif
1 Thu Nov 27 12:26:01 +0000 2025 kesalahan awal ini kecerobohan atau kelalaian ... negatif

In [ ]:
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix
import pandas as pd

# Get true labels from the manually labeled dataset
y_true = dg['Sentimen_2']

# Generate predictions for the same texts that were manually labeled
preds_for_labeled_data = labeler(dg['clean_text'].tolist())
y_pred = [label_map[p["label"]] for p in preds_for_labeled_data]

# Classification Report (Precision, Recall, F1, Accuracy)
print(classification_report(y_true, y_pred))

# Confusion Matrix
cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
print(cm)
precision recall f1-score support negatif 0.78 0.93 0.85 75 netral 0.85 0.79 0.81 70 positif 0.73 0.38 0.50 21 accuracy 0.80 166 macro avg 0.78 0.70 0.72 166 weighted avg 0.80 0.80 0.79 166 [[70 4 1] [13 55 2] [ 7 6 8]]
In [ ]:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d",
            xticklabels=['Pred Neg','Pred Net','Pred Pos'],
            yticklabels=['True Neg','True Net','True Pos'])
plt.show()

In [ ]:
from wordcloud import WordCloud
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from nltk.corpus import stopwords
import nltk
nltk.download('stopwords')

def plot_wordcloud(text, title):
    wc = WordCloud(width=800, height=500, background_color='white').generate(" ".join(text))
    plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
    plt.imshow(wc, interpolation='bilinear')
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.show()

# buat wordcloud untuk tiap cluster
for cluster in sorted(df1["label"].unique()):
    cluster_text = df1[df1["label"] == cluster]["clean_text"]
    plot_wordcloud(cluster_text, f"Wordcloud Cluster {cluster}")
[nltk_data] Downloading package stopwords to /root/nltk_data... [nltk_data] Unzipping corpora/stopwords.zip.
In [ ]:
#fungsi buat bikin donat chart
def donut(size, ax, angle=90, labels=None, colors=None, explode=None, shadow=None):

  #plot
  ax.pie(sizes, colors = colors, labels=labels, autopct='%.1f%%',
          startangle = angle  , pctdistance=0.8, explode = explode,
          wedgeprops=dict(width=0.4), shadow=shadow)

  #format
  plt.axis('equal')
  plt.tight_layout()

#plot arguments
sizes = df1.label.value_counts()

labels =["review Negatif","review Netral","review positif" ]
colors = ['#ff9999','#66b3ff','#99ff99']
exlplode = [0, 0, 0.1]

#create axes
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,8))

#plot donut
donut(sizes, ax, labels=labels, colors=colors, explode=exlplode, shadow=True)
ax.set_title('Distribusi Sentimen')

print(f"shape:{df.shape}")
df_count =df1["label"].value_counts()
print(df_count)
print('')


plt.show()
shape:(908, 15) label negatif 527 netral 314 positif 67 Name: count, dtype: int64
In [ ]:
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer

def top_ngram(klasifikasi, ngram_min=2, ngram_max=3, topn=20):
    text = df1[df1["label"] == klasifikasi]["clean_text"]
    vec = CountVectorizer(ngram_range=(ngram_min, ngram_max))
    ngram = vec.fit_transform(text)
    freq = ngram.sum(axis=0).A1
    vocab = {v: k for k, v in vec.vocabulary_.items()}
    top = freq.argsort()[::-1][:topn]
    return [(vocab[i], freq[i]) for i in top]

# contoh: tampilkan bigram/trigram
for c in sorted(df1["label"].unique()):
    print(f"\nBigram/Trigram label : {c}")
    print(top_ngram(c))
Bigram/Trigram label : negatif [('petugas kai', np.int64(100)), ('tumbler tuku', np.int64(96)), ('pegawai kai', np.int64(60)), ('perkara tumbler', np.int64(58)), ('kehilangan tumbler', np.int64(43)), ('kai nya', np.int64(40)), ('kai yang', np.int64(39)), ('tumbler yang', np.int64(39)), ('tanggung jawab', np.int64(37)), ('tumbler hilang', np.int64(36)), ('di pecat', np.int64(35)), ('di krl', np.int64(34)), ('kai dipecat', np.int64(34)), ('di kereta', np.int64(32)), ('gak ada', np.int64(29)), ('perusahaan kai', np.int64(29)), ('yang dipecat', np.int64(28)), ('di kai', np.int64(26)), ('gara gara', np.int64(26)), ('kasus tumbler', np.int64(24))] Bigram/Trigram label : netral [('kai commuter', np.int64(58)), ('kasus tumbler', np.int64(57)), ('di krl', np.int64(52)), ('tumbler tuku', np.int64(49)), ('tumbler hilang', np.int64(47)), ('hilang di', np.int64(46)), ('petugas kai', np.int64(40)), ('anita dewi', np.int64(38)), ('perusahaan kai', np.int64(38)), ('hilang di krl', np.int64(37)), ('kehilangan tumbler', np.int64(35)), ('tumbler penumpang', np.int64(27)), ('tumbler kai', np.int64(25)), ('tumbler hilang di', np.int64(24)), ('pemecatan petugas', np.int64(23)), ('media sosial', np.int64(22)), ('viral tumbler', np.int64(20)), ('di media', np.int64(20)), ('penumpang krl', np.int64(20)), ('di media sosial', np.int64(19))] Bigram/Trigram label : positif [('tumbler tuku', np.int64(12)), ('petugas kai', np.int64(9)), ('kai commuter', np.int64(8)), ('kai yang', np.int64(6)), ('kasus tumbler', np.int64(6)), ('mas kai', np.int64(5)), ('tumbler kai', np.int64(5)), ('ada yang', np.int64(5)), ('kai nya', np.int64(4)), ('di stasiun', np.int64(4)), ('tanggung jawab', np.int64(4)), ('tumbler ini', np.int64(4)), ('water station', np.int64(4)), ('pegawai kai', np.int64(4)), ('tumbler nya', np.int64(4)), ('cctv tumbler', np.int64(4)), ('di kai', np.int64(4)), ('bawa tumbler', np.int64(4)), ('prediksinya sini join', np.int64(3)), ('match malam ini', np.int64(3))]

from collections import Counter
import pandas as pd

top_per_label = {}

for lbl in df1["label"].unique():
    words = " ".join(df1[df1["label"] == lbl]["clean_text"]).split()
    counter = Counter(words).most_common(20)
    top_per_label[lbl] = counter

top_per_label
